note_data.title: Selection and evolution
keyPoints: All living organisms are made of cells and use ATP from respiration for energy-requiring processes
Eukaryotic cells contain a nucleus and membrane-bound organelles, while prokaryotic cells do not contain membrane-bound organelles and have DNA freely floating in cytoplasm
Plant cells have unique organelles including chloroplasts (site of photosynthesis), cell wall made of cellulose microfibrils, and vacuoles for support and storage
Animal cells may contain cilia for movement of substances and microvilli to increase surface area for absorption
Prokaryotic cells are unicellular, typically 1-5μm in diameter, with circular DNA, peptidoglycan cell walls, and smaller 70S ribosomes
Eukaryotic cells have linear DNA, larger 80S ribosomes, and various membrane-bound organelles like mitochondria, ER, and Golgi apparatus
Viruses are non-living structures consisting of nucleic acid (DNA or RNA) in a protein capsid, sometimes with a phospholipid envelope
Mitochondria and chloroplasts both contain small circular DNA and 70S ribosomes within eukaryotic cells

definitions.definition: A more detailed structure of cells that can be obtained by using a microscope
Cells that contain a nucleus and membrane-bound organelles
Unicellular organisms that do not contain membrane-bound organelles and have DNA freely floating in the cytoplasm
A protective protein coat that encloses nucleic acid in viruses

howToAnswer.instruction: Give one fact in one sentence. No explanation needed.
Give the point + reason using 'because' or 'this is because'
State clear differences between two concepts using 'whereas' or 'while'
State what happens in logical sequence without explaining why

modelAnswer.question: Explain how the founder effect can lead to speciation. [3]
modelAnswer.answer: A small group of individuals becomes separated from the main population and establishes a new population. This founding population has a reduced gene pool/genetic diversity compared to the original population. Over time, different mutations arise and different selection pressures act on the two populations, causing genetic divergence until reproductive isolation occurs.
markBreakdown: Mark 1: Small group separated/establishes new population
Mark 2: Reduced genetic diversity/gene pool in founding population
Mark 3: Genetic divergence leads to reproductive isolation/speciation

commonMistakes: Confusing the ribosome sizes - prokaryotes have 70S ribosomes while eukaryotes have 80S ribosomes in their cytoplasm
Forgetting that mitochondria and chloroplasts contain 70S ribosomes and circular DNA despite being in eukaryotic cells
Mixing up cell wall compositions - prokaryotes have peptidoglycan, plants have cellulose, and fungi have chitin
Thinking viruses are living organisms when they are non-living structures
examinerTips: Remember that centrioles are involved in cell division and are absent from most plant cells
Know that chloroplasts contain thylakoid membranes stacked to form grana and linked by lamellae
note_data.keyDiagramDescription: Graph showing normal distribution curves for directional, stabilising and disruptive selection, with arrows indicating direction of selection pressure and resulting population changes over time
pastPaperRefs: This topic was tested in M/J 2023 P2 Q4 (6 marks) — they asked students to explain how antibiotic resistance develops in bacteria through natural selection
N/D 2022 P1 Q15 (1 mark) — multiple choice question on which factor does NOT affect the rate of evolution in a population
M/J 2022 P2 Q6c (4 marks) — students had to describe how pesticide resistance could develop in a population of insects
N/D 2023 P2 Q3b (5 marks) — explain why some finches survived better than others during drought conditions on the Galapagos Islands
M/J 2021 P2 Q5a (3 marks) — define natural selection and give an example from nature

workedExample.question: Explain how a population of moths could evolve to become darker in color over several generations in an industrial area. (6 marks)
steps: Step 1: Identify the environmental pressure - industrial pollution creates dark, sooty surfaces
Step 2: Describe variation - moths show natural variation in color from light to dark
Step 3: Explain differential survival - dark moths are camouflaged better on sooty surfaces, so predators catch fewer dark moths than light ones
Step 4: State reproductive advantage - more dark moths survive to reproduce and pass on their genes
Step 5: Describe inheritance - dark color genes are passed to offspring
Step 6: Explain population change - over many generations, frequency of dark moths increases in the population
workedExample.answer: In industrial areas with pollution, dark moths have a survival advantage because they are better camouflaged against sooty surfaces compared to light moths. Predators are less likely to spot and eat dark moths. This means more dark moths survive to reproduce and pass on their genes for dark coloration to their offspring. Over many generations, the proportion of dark moths in the population increases while light moths become less common, resulting in evolution of the population toward darker coloration.
workedExample.markScheme: 1 mark each for: environmental pressure/selection pressure mentioned, variation in population, differential survival/reproduction, inheritance of characteristics, change in population over time, plus 1 mark for clear explanation

examinerWarnings: Examiner Report 2023: 'Many candidates confused evolution with adaptation of individuals' — always explain that individuals adapt but populations evolve over generations
Examiner Report 2022: 'Students often forgot to mention that characteristics must be inherited' — always state that beneficial traits are passed from parents to offspring through genes
Examiner Report 2023: 'Candidates frequently wrote that organisms 'decide to evolve' or 'try to adapt' — remember that natural selection is not conscious and organisms do not choose to evolve
note_data.keyDiagram: Draw a flow chart showing the process of natural selection: Start with 'Population with variation' (show mixed colored circles), arrow to 'Environmental pressure' (predator or climate), arrow to 'Some individuals better adapted survive' (show fewer circles, mostly one color), arrow to 'Survivors reproduce' (circles with offspring), arrow to 'Beneficial alleles passed on' (DNA symbols), ending with 'Population composition changes over time' (mostly one color circles). Include labels for each stage and use different colors to show changes in population composition.
note_data.quickRecap: Cells are the basic units of all living organisms and can be classified as prokaryotic or eukaryotic based on their internal organization. Eukaryotic cells have membrane-bound organelles including a nucleus, while prokaryotic cells have simpler structures without membrane-bound organelles. Plant and animal cells have specific adaptations like chloroplasts and cilia respectively, while viruses are non-living structures containing genetic material in protein coats.
